channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 70 replies — 179.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 70 • Updated: 2026-06-01 16:02 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290849
>>62290812
cool TP
yeah let me know
this apartment is way better than the last place I was assigned thank fuck
kekeke we'll see you next thread ig but worth it lel
jannies might have chilled tho I hope

62290910
>>62290812
LEGEND!

I'm gonna need the name of this goth whore next. Are those braces?

62291155
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
Fucking hell, TP. More of this please, especially >>62290355

62291188
>>62285628
Nope. Sold everything when it became clear they’re not actually interested in building utility for the token. It’s a memecoin connected to a browser. They had the world by the balls and they made a conscious decision to do absolutely nothing for BAT. They cannot be trusted

62291222
>>62290355
You're a pornographer, the fuck you care?

62291254
>>62286450
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
TP MOGS /gif/
THE MOTHERFUCKING GOAT

62291656
>>62290812
Wonderful rack on her. Lets see some asian MILFS GILFS

62291660
>>62287337
she's hands down the hottest crypto girl, the hottest thing about her is that she hides how big her ass is, won't even show it off out of modesty

62291667
>>62289174
Most BAT holders moved on to something else eventually, but still hold some BAT out of loyalty and the purest copium you'll ever find

Anyone who was "all-in" on BAT has sold or killed themselves by now

62292238
>>62286450
need more of this one lads

62292673
>>62291660
i want to see her tiddies so bad

62292929
>>62290849
>this apartment is way better than the last place I was assigned
I'm happy for you ^_^!

>>62290910
>Are those braces?
Yes sir!

>>62291155
Czech'd and I should be around to post more later tonight for you.

>>62291254
That means more than you know, lad! Thanks for the love <3

>>62291656
>Lets see some asian MILFS GILFS
Short on time rn, but if you want something better than this, lmk and I'll see what I can do later.

62293156
>>62292929
>Czech'd and I should be around to post more later tonight for you.

oh baby. Trans jans are letting TP poast freely for once in a blue moon, love to see it

62293250
>>62285628
i've still got 20k BAT, might swap for LINK if it pumps

62293268
>>62290812
that's def a prosthetic vest

62293317
>>62293268
You’re a retard

62293375
>>62293317
im right faggot

62293393
>>62293375
Keep telling yourself nigger

62293407
>>62292929
>tfw no chink grandma gf

62294009
bat is the biggest fucking let down in the crypto world a fucking regret ever fucking hearing about bat

62294011
and faggot brendan is piece of shit con man no better than a jeet

62294012
>>62294009
>a fucking regret ever fucking hearing about bat

We all do, these threads are our group therapy cope session. Welcome

62294071
>>62294009
Dude just let go sell it for something that will actually go up in price. I know it’s hard but if you actually look around there’s companies out there that are still actually looking to improve peoples lives thru crypto with actual utility unlike BAT which is a nothing more than a glorified memecoin. VVV and RAIL are two perfect examples that are mark to market. There’s more than that. Think ai, privacy, stablecoins and robotics. BAT is fucking cooked

62295627
>>62292929
Get back here TP we need moarrrrrrrrr thots

62296078
>>62294011
birendra eichwala and his do not redeem token

62296132
we need the poster of thots

62296359
>>62294009
>>62294011
we should sue him for false advertising and stealing investors money

62297106
tps last post got wiped out. It was fun while it lasted

62297187
>>62296359
yes

62299271
>>62286450
busty redhead please.
I am suddenly bulilsh on BAT

item.title: who are these types of homes built for? >$390k to share a wall with a stranger >HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on li… — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
who are these types of homes built for? >$390k to share a wall with a stranger >HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on li…

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-01 16:20 UTC

OP

who are these types of homes built for?
>$390k to share a wall with a stranger
>HOA fee exists but isn't disclosed on listing
>no trees, no privacy shrubs, living there will put you on SSRIs
>undoubtedly built by drunken illiterate retard spics in 3 weeks
>costs 20% more than a SFH existing build (with trees, a lawn, NO SHARED WALLS, etc.)
you'd need to be the world's biggest goy retard to consider these.

Early replies (first 3)

62298165
for people dealing in the real world

other questions?

62298175
>>62298161
>no trees, no privacy shrubs,
If only the buyer could add those himself later, choosing whatever he prefers.

Oh well, sadly that's impossible.

62298195
>>62298161
everything about these is absolutely dreadful. it feels like you're living in an apartment (except you're on the hook for $390k over 30 years + HOA + property taxes + tip.)
>GRAY, GRAY, GRAY
>layout designed by someone with downs syndrome + FAS
yeah uh i want a single basin sink island that's barely large enough to fit a cutting board and i want it in the middle of my OPEN CONCEPT gaynigger living room + kitchen (where is the dining room? who the fuck knows) with a view of my barren wasteland front yard.

WAIT -- I'M NOT DONE -- give me a bunch of ugly ass light fixtures and drywall features to cover the oversized beam that supports the open concept ceiling. and i DO NOT want crown molding on my ugly ass gray walls because that would add too much to the cost.

oh, and can you make the stairwell barely wide enough to fit an adult? i want it to be FUCKING IMPOSSIBLE to move furniture or appliances in or out of my GOYBOX.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298218
oh yeah btw, this is not a house or an apartment

this is a birdhouse because its made out of plywood

even my stray cats have a stronger shed than your average ameripoor

62298222
relax sperg they are houses for poors

62298225
>>62298218
WAIT A MINUTE I'M NOT DONE WITH MY LIST OF DEMANDS. can you make my bathroom tiny and then cram a HIS + HIS (because only a retarded FAGGOT would live here) double sink in there? also the fixtures need to be matte black because i want it to look fucking HORRIBLE if i ever repaint the walls to something not retarded.

62298231
>>62298225
forgot my pic.
>>62298222
why would a poor person spend 10-20% more money than a comparable single family home with no HOA and lower property taxes?

62298240
>American anon discovers non-detached new build housing
Welcome to the 70s in Europe

62298242
>>62298231
Because it's the other way around it's cheaper than a detached.

62298247
>>62298222
Let OP cook, this is the best thread I’ve seen on biz in weeks.

62298249
have the people who designed these types of "homes" ever heard of ceiling fans? i've never once seen a ceiling fan in a new build.
>umm actually you're just supposed to run your chinese factory seconds Heat Pump™ 24/7 until it blows up 3 hours outside the warranty period from being deliberately undersized to save on build costs.

62298255
>>62298242
do you have a learning disability?
https://www.realtor.com/realestateandhomes-search/Pennsylvania/type-single-family-home/55p-hide/price-na-400000/sby-6?view=map&pos=40.437769,-75.548192,40.302008,-75.410677,13&qdm=true

62298258
>>62298161
please americans do not build semis they are disgusting inhuman domiciles and they plague they uk housing market
i can tell you that there are people, vile subhuman philistines, who actually like having a semi because they consider it neighbourly but those people ought to be put in camps where they can enjoy a "neighbourly" dormitory

62298260
>>62298255
I don't know why becasue I don't care to look into it but there's a reason that it's more expensive.

62298263
Jeets

62298273
>>62298242
mmm, yes, yes, i see -- but these single family homes don't appeal to my goyish sensibilities. where's the smell of ethnic food seeping through my paper thin walls that i share with a brown family of 6? and all of this... what do you call it, grass and trees? i'm expected to mow that myself???

62298310
>>62298260
yes anon, materials and labor purchased today are notoriously cheaper than material and labor purchased 30 years in the past. excavating and paving dozens of acres of raw land and running hundreds of feet of water/sewer to a public tie-in is also cheaper than buying on a plot of land already tied into public utilities with a much shorter run.
>YEAHH UHH I WANT A DRIVEWAY.... BUT LIKE, I WANT IT BARELY LARGE ENOUGH TO PARK A SUBCOMPACT CROSSOVER.

62298384
not having trees is a plus... don't have to deal with cleaning leafs in the falls and don't have to deal with bird shit and tree sap messing up your car paint

i can tell none of you faggots have ever had to rake leaves

62298404
>>62298384
>not having to do 2 hours of work a week 3 months out of the year is worth living in a barren wasteland
no thanks, I’ll keep my trees

62298408
>>62298384
GOY spotted. raking leaves is a mentally ill suburban boomer phenomena. i'm puzzled by the scenario in your goybrain where trees are apparently only to be planted in areas that hang over your car? also why is your car not parked in the garage? (i know the answer. it's because you're a mentally ill suburban boomer. your garage isn't for parking vehicles, it's packed floor to ceiling with 1500 square feet of worthless nicknacks and other retarded garbage.)

62298416
The people that buy these are plebs who know nothing about basic construction/being handy, and associate anything "new" as being superior. True, there is no lead or asbestos, but the place will be falling apart within 20 years, and it is inhuman to pay that much to live in such close proximity. Homes from the 60s-70s have much better quality materials and larger lot sizes.

62298431
>>62298384
>>62298408
>OH YEAH, A RAKE? AND A LAWNMOWER? WHERE AM I GONNA KEEP THAT? I GOT NO ROOM IN MY GARAGE.
>LISTEN -- LISTEN, I KNOW IT'S CALLED A ONE "CAR" GARAGE BUT.... THAT'S JUST AN EXPRESSION!!!! A FIGURE OF SPEECH!!!!!

62298446
>>62298384
>>62298408
>>62298431
>don't you -- don't you FUCKING touch that motorcycle tire!!!!!
>it is NOT garbage!!!!!

62298546
>>62298195
>>62298207
>>62298273
>>62298310

OOOOOOO He's priced out!

Don't feel bad rentoid, I own multiple properties and also would not buy that Overpriced HOA shit.

There's a point that the younger generation has to realize what the boomers did. DIY while being totally unqualified to do it, ignore all the faggy permitting and regulations, build your own if you own the land.

62298565
>>62298546
who said i'm priced out? i make $150k and i'm looking at homes that cost $350-400k. i work from home so i have a lot of flexibility.

62298583
>>62298161
This thing looks either a made up fake computer graphic or ikea the house

62298588
read OPs posts with Nick Rochefort's voice

62298787
>>62298565

If true, wtf are you so close to Niggerdelphia?

Need easy access to ur trans hookers?

Real estate has always been about the location dawg.

62298816
>>62298310
>>62298161
These are european style shitboxes why the fuck are you having them?

62298828
>>62298787
niggerdelphia is an hour away. i might be LAID off one day, so i want to stay close to jobs that pay more than $16/hour.

62299240
Buying a house that has been made within the last 15 years is retarded. Guaranteed to have many problems.

62299332
>>62298161
Peak goybox.

62299341
>>62298175
If it is indeed part of a HOA, it is impossible

item.title: IT BEGINS — 30 replies — 120.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT BEGINS

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-01 16:20 UTC

OP

THE PONZI IS COLLAPSING

Early replies (first 3)

62298640
Everything crashes and Link goes to $10,000

62298646
>>62298640
I sneed this to happen.

62298648
the most pathetic is that he had to sell 32 BTC

like what s happening Michael, you need some spare change?

you can give us your cashapp bro

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298652
>>62298640
Link is going to $2

THE cuckolds of crypto

62298664
>>62298627
Does btc even have enough liquidity for him to sell? He’ll have to slowly sell over like 2 years to exit completely, or he could market sell and red candle btc to 10k

62298679
>>62298648
>Michael "I’m not giving my wallet addresses because that’s insecure" Saylor

If your QI > 115, you can see how bullshit the whole thing is

62298693
>>62298679
IQ not QI (Frenchfag here)

62298706
>>62298679
i know this guy smell like scam

i cant wait for his ponzi to crash so bitcoin can have its bottom and go back to new ATH

62298735
>>62298706
That would be brutal, but that’s the brutality we need desu

62298767
>>62298706
>so bitcoin can have its bottom and go back to new ATH
You realise with all the shit happening in the world right now bitcoin should right now be already in the hundreds of thousands or even millions, the fact it has been crabbing then crashing shows its going down and never going back up.

62298771
>>62298664
wet dream

62298778
>>62298648
Definitely a test to gauge the markets reaction before doing bigger sells

62298782
>>62298706
total mass extinction event, literally 10x worser than FX collapse, you know that anon, right?

62298784
>>62298627
Did he actually sell some?

Lol, if so. He's run out of new greater fools.

62298795
>>62298778
This

62298882
>>62298782
The only worse thing would be Satoshi rising from his grave like Altered Beast and dumping his cheeky stack lol

Yeah, a Saylor demise would be really bad, but better now than later. Letting one single person/company have so much ascendency over a decentralised asset, to use it as pseudo-collateral for a fucking ponzi to boot, can’t end well anyway.

62298898
>>62298627

>it’s decentralized currency
>held mainly by hedge funds and government black budget shell companies

62298932
>>62298679
This is the best point that everyone just ignores because they want number go up…not the answer I would want to hear from a prospective investor’s perspective

62298968
Good, all ponzis come to an end. Sell the rest of your crapto and invest in US stocks.

62298985
>>62298778
Agree, I mean, why the heck would he transfer 400 bitcoin to Coinbase, withdraw and only sell 32?

Bitcoin already reacted bad on Friday with this rumour, if bitcoin dropped this bad selling only 32, imagine when he sells 10-100x that amount

62299055
>>62298627
nigga is faking his buys
theyre gonna make moves about him while hes in prison

62299198
>>62298679
only a madman would give out his wallet addresses
t.iq 118

62299214
>>62298778
This anon gets it

62299267
>>62298627
>THE PONZI IS COLLAPSING
No, SPX is fine, it didn't collapse.

62299279
>>62298627
OH SHIT IM DUMPING IT

TRIPLE TOP, POP, ITS DONE
247 trading dumping through your folio
How I shorted the top never stopped you lost control
Dumping in a blaze of fire, candle through my bones
OH SHIT IM DUMPING IT

Out of the green, ending the pump
Bobo coming through like fuck did I hear a swan
Man I don't know but it made you so depleted
Shorts creeping up without showing you jack
Sudden pumps from below, you think you got the moon
You're riding that green until I sell too soon
You're coming back slurping that dip
Save your money wagie, I'm downsizing your shift

OH SHIT IM DUMPING IT
Hell yeah fuck yeah I feel like bogging him
Alright that's tight, what it's like to profit this

Bear market shot down your chart like a hellish flame
Choke on the wreckage exhale your acceptance
Permabulls are so fucking weird
Trying to lighten the mood over here
But they can't cause the wall's way too sheer
My red candle melting zeros so hard you call me a seer

Lower highs and lower lows
Fuck what you thought thought summer meant
This bubble popping I slammed my dick in it
Sell while you can or bleed like the damned
Ain't a chart zoomed out enough to catch this
Knife straight to zero, no more heroes
Satoshi is dead and you swim in red

TAKING BULLTARDS DOWN
ALL I SEE ARE CANDLES
FOMOBRAINS TRYING TO GET A HANDLE
STUDY THE CHARTS THIS IS THE MEGABART

62299284
>>62299279
OH SHIT IM DUMPING IT
Hell yeah fuck yeah I feel like bogging him
Alright that's tight, what it's like to profit this

Lunar eclipse analysis? Bull.
Shit. Bitch it's 10AM and I'm phasing it
Out. Time to buy, you want to die?
See your life flash in this crash
I'm loading up all in cash I mash
Sell I bash that market cap
Watch this leverage thrash
You should have stayed away from this scene
Not your day too late to pray for green
Screens redraw the lines new all time high was just a dream

Wake and bake now it hits
Years of pay poured into shit
Coins those heavy bags
Damn you thought that was a bull flag
Wave it white dumping past your strike
Priced in you allege you're out of cash
Flow only scraps left to dredge
Up that bump? You made your call
Out of your mind, out of time too
Go back to school you motley fool

LESSON 1, TRADING TIPS
START WITH THE ALPHA BET
WHAT YOU BOUGHT AINT NO DIP
THE 1 YEAR CHART, A SCAM WICK
YOU THOUGHT YOU BOUGHT THE DIP

OH SHIT IM DUMPING IT

62299301
>>62299198
Also he refused to be independently audited (keeping them secret)…dumbass

62299309
>>62299267
soon my fren, really soon

62299323
Kidneys dawg

62299347
>>62298627
The sale is being used to fund STRC div., I'd wager that they were likely sold from a tax loss advantaged tranche. They "lost" money on it to help with taxes.

Two birds, one stone.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 157 replies — 69.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 157 • Updated: 2026-06-01 14:09 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

62290856
>>62271933
There's truth to this, but I dunno about 40k. Buy "dips" now I say

62290931
>>62272803
>>62272874

The conundrum that niggers on here don't understand is that everyone wants upside volatility, but it's the volatility itself that ultimately creates the ceiling.

It's the cute naive chick that ceases to be so once you fuck her. You can't have your cute and fuck it too.

62291823
>>62290931
You're also another nigger

62292614
>>62287393
So how much are you making?

62292863
>>62271712
the absolute best case scenario for bitcoin would be a 3x. there are other coins currently on the market that can and probably will give me a higher roi.

62292885
>>62271712
Someday a scalable bitcoin will fork away from BTC with enough ideological support to effectively upgrade what everyone will agree to be bitcoin. Then I will buy bitcoin. Until then it's pointless to buy a coin just to get the real bitcoin eventually as a dividend fork. Just wait and buy scalable bitcoin when it exists.

62292989
>>62271712
>saylor holding 5% (more due to lost coins)
>trump stink
>american hegemony stink
>quantum fud
>couldnt even act as a hedge during a war which is its only job
>2028 gary gensler revenge tour
Wasn't the plan this whole time to dump on the institutions? The OGs dumped billions and billions of market clips on them and left forever. That was the entire goal and it was accomplished, genuinely wtf more do you want? The win-con was fulfilled, if you didn't make it then come up with a new one or find another asset. Or you can continue DCAing for the next 30 years I guess

62292997
What are you getting, a 2-4x depending on where the bottom is?

62294119
>>62292863
And what is it? Low cap alts?

62294591
>>62271960
They will say the same thing when it hit 200K Kek. Honestly BTC has a lot of potential most especially since only 1% is in Defi, Imagine how huge it will be with TBV now in play.

62295602
>>62292997
Yeah, that's it.

62295607
>>62272678
Under 20k? Are you sure?

62295716
>>62271712
>What’s stopping you from buying BTC?
the existence of Monero

62295989
>>62274282
Why the hate

62296012
>>62295607
sure under 20k
why whats your get back in number?

62297371
>>62296012
Not under 20k

62297376
>>62295716
Ah privacy ok ok

62298604
>>62294591
You're right anon

62298612
>>62292989
Well, I'm not afraid of short term volatility

item.title: THE cuckolds of investments — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE cuckolds of investments

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-01 16:15 UTC

OP

.

Early replies (first 3)

62299110
>>62299081
imagine buying saylorcoin when you could have bought ANY other asset in the past year

hes LITERALLY forced to sell and has 800,000 to go

62299165
>>62299110
Didnt Grayscale sold something like 500k btc?

62299252
>>62299081
I FUCKING HATE CRYPTO AND ITS SHILLS
BUY REAL STOCKS LIKE A MAN

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62299256
>>62299252
Is Nestle stock ok to buy?

62299266
>>62299252
why not short it? It's once in 4 year time opportunity, you can literally make a bank with it

62299292
>>62299081
It dumped one too many time to kill LINK momentum

Not to mention that whole sub 100k dump was because of smartcon

BTC is dead.

62299300
Im holding HYPE and still up on the daily. Fuck BTC

62299307
>>62299266
because crypto is the most volatile thing there is. you are 100% gambling. I don't bother with crypto so I don't know, but do people do arbitrage with it?

62299315
>>62299252
Thanks, I will now buy Bitcoin ETF, sir.

62299316
>>62299081
holy fuck btc is getting SHIT ON

62299319
>>62299307
Really difficult depending on the market. The good thing about crypto is that it respects a lot TA and the 4 year cycle, you can use that to your favor

item.title: How can all of us get rich, not some but ALL of us? — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How can all of us get rich, not some but ALL of us?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-01 16:14 UTC

OP

How can all of us get rich, not some but ALL of us?

Early replies (first 3)

62299125
>>62299093
Hyperinflation

>a banana is $15,000,000,000 but WE BILLIONAIRES NOW

62299173
>>62299093
Who is us? As long us is not too big we can get rich if Elon gifts us money.

62299179
It's simple.

*lip smack*

We kill the Batman.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62299194
>>62299093
for me, it's kibshi

62299200
>>62299173
Asking Elon for money is against the board rules so we must find a different means of getting all of us to make it

62299201
>>62299179
If its so simple why havent you done it already?

62299245
>>62299093
The people who have money are the boomers which are in stocks and derivatives. Crypto is dying out and was a failed experiment.

62299260
>>62299200
it's really telling how completely poor and retarded someone is when they complain about Elon hoarding wealth because even if he could magically liquidate 100% of his NW are current market valu (impossible) it would be like $2k for every american. Real life changing money

62299262
>>62299201
I never do things I can do for free

62299317

62298520

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298521
>>62298516
Bless her heart

62298564
>>62298516
>>62298520
If you did this 1 year ago you'd be stinking rich now.

62299000
>>62298564
Most of the time it doesn't work. That's why almost nobody did it. Paradigm was that ram prices will go down long term.

62299073
>>62298266
you're right:
>everyone hates you and you're remembered for years as that scalping opportunistic asshole who profited off computer enthusiasts. you have more money but you've contributed to the denial of access of empowering hardware to others, including people who directly could have had a positive impact on you (or others, who then directly interact with you).
you're wrong:
>data centers liquidate, flooding the market with insanely cheap hardware. people can breath again. meanwhile, you've 0.1x'd
just don't do it. the risk/reward is neither worth it, nor is you aligning yourself against the rest of humanity.

62299324
>>62299073
>>data centers liquidate, flooding the market with insanely cheap hardware.
Literally impossible. Compute is always needed especially now.

item.title: How is it that everyone on reddit are millionaires — 36 replies — 23.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is it that everyone on reddit are millionaires

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-01 14:55 UTC

OP

>excluding crypto
Seriously, do you all have more than 200k in your brokerage accounts? Its crazy to me that you Americans are that well off. Even on ticktok live streams, you see this guy presenting his 200k+ portfolio in his mid to late 20s. Others on reddit are showcasing their portfolio at mid 30 already reaching 1M+. Yet people all around me are claiming that life has gotten increasingly harder and more expensive. Here is Switzerland we are getting drained with increasing Krankenkasse (health care) costs and mandatory radio-service billings (Serafe) to the point that we are voting to decrease these this month, somehow.
This cant all be fake personal marketing and fake boasting, can it?
Statistics show that the median savings in top wealth western nations is like 50-80k (checking, savings and investments, excluding house, cars etc.) in total.
ALSO...WHERE IS ALL THIS MONEY COMING FROM TO INVEST IN THE STOCK MARKET? How are these companies being pumped so heavily with cash that giga-corps like Micron reach 1 trillion easily overnight?

Early replies (first 3)

62295606
>>62295508
It's called consolidation. There's very few websites of any note and only a few kooks in the developed world are consciously not online so if you're rich you're gonna be in one or two communities.

62295628
>>62295508
??? it's a bull market

62295633
>>62295508
>obsessed with americans backwoods eurohillbilly
lol

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62296160
200k is not that much if you're a single man with a decent job and you don't waste money you can easily build a 200k portfolio in 3 years even if you're just DCA into indices.

62296173
Nobody flexing their $20000 poorfolio. Also yeah dummy, the FIRE subreddit (and other financial subreddits) tend to have more monetarily successful people

62296180
>>62295508
Also bro you could have googled this in like 5 seconds. Retard

62296281
>>62295508
they live with their parents, meaning their parents are millionaires, not them

or their parents passed on some of their wealth to them. There's a lot of Gen X and boomer millionaires here since all you had to do was buy a house in a moderately popular part of the US and you're probably now a millionaire.

or the people on reddit are much older than you expect. For example I'm a millionaire and have been for 20+ years now, but I am also almost 60 years old now. People on 4chan assume I must be lying about money because they can't imagine a 60 year old boomer posting on 4chan.

62296414
>check'em
P.S. Git Gud.

62296439
>>62295737
>Switzerland
>europoor thirdie

62296451
>>62296281
Until proven otherwise, I expect the age of any poster in forums (including 4chan) or newspaper comment sections to be between 50 and 70, desu

Everything, literally everything makes sense once you read with this assumption in mind

62296456
>>62295508
Its called working and investing your money into an index fund. I know its fucking crazy but its actually easy being a millionaire if you are patient.

62296458
because stocks are on an absolute tear, and none of these zoomers are doing crypto which just takes.

62296494
>>62296458
True, tech stocks are the place for retarded money today (just left curve it bro, stop having thoughts and shit)

>if they're retarded, how come they make tons of money and you don't? lol
They're going to finish like they did in crypto in 2017-2022, and in online poker in 2005-2011: mostly broke, because they're fish on a heater

62296574
Just put in a little effort gay ass nigga its not hard to be better than 99% of people, I think im even top 2-3% earner or at least close to it but then you need to realize thats just the start its wild how much some people actually make, couple million is nothing.

62296579
>>62296456
kek'd

the average age for someone working and investing in index funds to become a millionaire is well past 60.

62296588
>>62296579
As I said above, expect the age of any forum loudmouth, even on /biz/, to be between 50 and 70; then, it makes sense

If it sounds like a boomer, it's probably a boomer

62296592
>>62295508
200k is not that crazy. Anyone can have that if they go to college, get a 100k job and live with their parents for a few years.

62296646
>>62295508
Switzerland is richer than the US

62296656
>>62296588
yep, if you're offended by people disagreeing with you in your hugbox but love to come online and say retarded things all day, you will probably encounter some loudmouths and boomers. Old people don't give a fuck about your fragile feelings.

62297538
>>62296149
Here's a (You) and a Jeffy. I won't let your hard work go unnoticed.

62297541
>>62295508
This is me. Must suck to be a Europoor or a cryptocuck.

62297556
>>62296149
intredisting

62297559
>>62297541
Pwease... anon. Pwease help a nigguh out.

62297566
>>62297541
You are part of the 0.1 . possible to ha've earned it through specific and hard work

62297591
>>62297566
In America, this places me not quite in the top 1%. Need to get to 14 million for top 1%.

Got a tech job in 2011. Six figure income plus six figure equity compensation package. Get a promotion and yearly pay increases and bonuses along the way. Keep expenses low, invest more than 70% of income per year and keep stock as the RSUs vest. Max out tax deferred investment accounts.

Enjoyed a few good bull markets in FAGMAN stocks, 2017 crypto bull, Covid bull run, and AI/GPUs/memory.

62297638
Ignoring the larpers who claim they made trillions from the quajillion market or whatever the answer is that retardditors don't have a culture of being a bagholding dumbass/schizo permabear who fuds up and coming projects or stocks for literally years on end while they're running up. The sheer amount of seethe right now at, say, HYPE which is up about 11x from its launch a year and a half ago demonstrates how gay and stupid this board is. The seething at Micron for literally 5 of the last 6 months, until it DECISIVELY crossed 1 trillion and then everyone claimed they "had calls on it" (no proofs provided). Because of the way /biz/ is you can have the same dumb faggots spamming fud or their dumb baggie/bear threads every day endlessly while reddit's threads exist, are selfcontained and people with usernames and an agenda can be ignored . They simply aren't able to constantly spam unfounded fud and basically fuck the entire board out of making it on a good coin or stock because they've got a dumb short that they think they can make if they spam their stupid shit often enough, or a bag they're married to.

Ironic. /biz/ culture of chaos originally enabled new projects to spring up and be shilled rapidly but this has no become a source of weakness, while not being able to be a spamming permabear dumbass has enabled redditors to accumulate good projects and make actual money.

>>62297591
sneed

62297869
>>62297638
I invested in micron like 4 months ago off advice from here and am officially in the black on my portfolio

62297883
Reddit users are typical SF cucks working tech, with salaries of 200k+ and stock options in an insane tech boom its hard NOT to be a millionaire after a few years

62297890
>>62295508
Most of Europe has gotten poorer. If you have your shit together the US is a far better place to be.

>Statistics show that the median savings in top wealth western nations is like 50-80k

Yeah, are you such a brainlet to not recognize that's the median and that the guy who wants to brag about his savings and focuses on finance might be a 1 in 20 guy?

High paying jobs in the US just don't really exist in Europe. A 24 year old lawyer in big law is going to be at $250,000 after bonus for his starting pay and making probably close to $500k by 30, if he makes partner he'll be earning $10MM+ a year. An AI engineer will make $500k-100MM+ a year. Surgeons will make $1M-$10MM in a lot of fields and locations.

Lots of Americans don't earn that or blow their money on pick-up trucks, but the European mind cannot comprehend American prosperity

62297936
>>62297890
The tradeoff is having to live here and not comfy eurolands
always grass (appears) greener

62298065
>>62295508
>How is it that everyone on reddit are millionaires
Have you not considered reddit is a communist marxist psyop? You've seen how controlled they are over there and how anything said thats negative gets down voted into oblivion, plus anything thats promoting anything thats outside the leftist/progressive narrative gets banned right away and shut down.

The reason why theres a lot of money floating around over there is because half the place is leftist marxist spooks who are bankrolled to condition and push people were they want them to be: Digital

62298085
>>62297566
>source from 2015

62298922
>>62295508

OP

You were too late for bitcoin

you got psychologically destroyed by millennial and gen x goobers posting on twitter from their rented condos in miami

And now you're here trying to tiktok dance your way into fucken millions

GTFO

62293753

62293756
>>62293753

*does the fortnite dance*

feed my more low i.q. shortform slop sir!

62293763
>>62293756
imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293934
>>62293920
anyone who is spiritually miami deserved to be crushed, that is true.

i'm just some bloke who used tism pattern recognition and intuition to financially help himself and his family. i'm not sorry for any of it and i'd do it all again. i pay my tax like a good goy and don't have to wagie because of this, literally every day i'm free to do as i please. it was worth it desu. take risks in life. doesn't have to be crypto, fuck crypto if you hate it. just bet on yourself in some avenue of life. risk it all.

62293943
>>62293934
for sure, those are great points. And well done!

62294000
>>62293753
What's a non boomer business?

62294194
Chainlink solves multiple real world problems. If you're too dumb to see that, maybe lurk a little longer and read more.
More generally, cryptography, and the financial instruments being developed that are secured by it, strengthens capitalism. It gives more tools to more people and increases the efficiency of the economy. Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money. If you can't appreciate that, you should suck your double barrel that's on your nightstand and get the fuck off of my board you decrepit, balding abomination

62294197
>>62293749
Ok newfag

62294203
>>62293749
The wirld is only getting more digital. It only makes sense that digital assets would raise in value. Right now it's a free for all casino. Take it or leave it this is the way it is.

62294208
>>62293826
>i've roundtripped low 7figs
>pokemon cards
The only thing you've roundtripped is the methheads behind the dumpster.

62294210
>>62293749
t top buying baggie.

62294219
>>62294208
>>pokemon cards
i think you're confusing anon here, i never mentioned anything about trading cards.

62294221
>>62294219
My mistake. You are still a homosexual zoomer for investing in shitcoins.

62294231
>>62294221
wow, that was a very rude thing to say.

62294248
>>62293749
>crypto shills destroyed /biz/
/biz/ was created specifically to contain crypto shilling

62294250
>>62294194
>Chainlink solves multiple real world problems.
stopped reading right there, baggie

62294305
>>62294194
>Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money
Explain what this means. Seriously, explain it. Tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. How is that any different than buying 'gold' on an exchange that's just a certificate? They're both digital, but one of them is just stored on a rube-goldberg mechanism instead of a simple database. How does it enable "the whole world" to have exposure to hard money?

You're just saying fucking words.

62294322
>>62294305
>how are tokenized assets different from legacy assets??

t.

62294326
>>62294322
Yes. In a qualitative way, not a quantitative way, explain how they are fundamentally different. Being stored on a slightly different digital substrate doesn't change : you hold a -promise- that you own gold, not actual physical gold.

62294328
>>62294326
Ask your local AI fuckhead.
Nobody here wants to explain the absolute basics of tokenization to you, even the banks have been trying to explain the benefits for over half a decade.

62294331
>>62294328
I want to see you explain it, because you can't, without regurgitating another explanation that will be fille with just as much of a smokescreen.

62294337
>>62294331
listen to yourself

62294340
>>62294337
>a really simple example for you
You're the one who said it. If it's so simple, surely you can bang out a coherent explanation in a minute or two.

You're a tech fetishist. You are mentally bound to this retarded world because you imagine it makes you smart, but it's really just smart in the "grandma thinks he's good with computer" kind of way.

62294365
>>62294340
Take a deep breath you fat boomer, your bp is spiking.

62294367
>>62293749
These sluts must know they are creating wankmaterial. They arent fooling anyone. Skanks. I hate it when they trick my brain into wanting to put my penis inside them. They would be raped 24/7 in the old days.

62294368
>>62294365

62294378
>>62293749

62294446

62294649

62294739

item.title: When are PC parts going to be affordable again? — 42 replies — 16.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
When are PC parts going to be affordable again?

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-06-01 13:51 UTC

OP

When are PC parts going to be affordable again? I'm tired of not being able to play games made in this decade.

Early replies (first 3)

62294333
>>62294325
Stop playing games you child

62294453
>>62294325
Not sure but Macbook Neo seems reasonably priced to me.

62294457
>>62294333
This. You should be focusing on activities that will lift you up from not being able to afford PC parts.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294665
>>62294654
I invested in amd and nvidia in 2019 so, yeah.

62294669
>>62294325
If you haven't bought spare gaming pcs after seeing what happened to NIVIDIA GPUs, it's honestly on you.
I saw the writing in the wall and bought a cheap gaming pc with an AMD GPU for $700 on black friday and just held on to it just in case my current one breaks so I probably have 10-20 years before having to get a new one.

62294681
>>62294665
ok so we have the same opinion that this retard should have invested?

62294719
>Might have to save up for an extra another couple of months for gayming machine
Aww diddums

62294724
How old are you people?
Is this board filled with teens?
fuck your video games, grow up you useless fat incel faggot

62294742
>>62294453

I was watching a raid array rebuild and zening out and it hit me, normies like you, apple marketing shill spammers, you will never be able to have an offline LAN

You are too stupid and lazy. It's simply beyond you.The reasons why you would need one are incomprehensibe to someone who thinks brands are status symbols. The fact that LAN cables don't have a brand that signifies status to fools means you can't even understand why LAN cables exist or who would want one. I mean you know you are lying and selling awful underpowered overpriced 8gb uninspired thrash that traps consumers into an awful corporate walled garden cloud portal devices hell but you are so lacking ability, characater, morals, technical ability you are willing to spam this junk for low wages. That's your life. You probably spend most of it on food and rent and then use the free apple homosexual mating call product you get to sit in starbucks trying to hit on other men, hoping some elderly faggot will adopt you and you can live with them as a carer/prositute in exchange for some near geriatric ejaculating in your anus. Even if you won a lottery you would spend it all on overpriced, poor value brandslop logos and subscriptions, the little brain you have was completely overpowered by modern marketing, so much so you ended up a minion of it.

You're the perfect example of the future digital slave, you will have to subscribe to get access to remote storage, cloud games, music, movies and computing resources accessed via terrible tiny screened, overpriced devices with awful specs deliberately designed to be useless for anything other that cloud access portals. Yet you will proudly display your slave brand logo as if telling the world you are a good slave before you go and let some geriatric ejaculate in your anus for food and then spend any time you have left spamming apple marketing idiocy in desperation to get another free sample because it means everything to you to own gank brandslop

62294743
>>62294333
I refuse there people over the age of 21 who still play video games who aren't influencers paid to do it

62294746
>>62294681
yeah, only normalfags didn't find it obvious
we had pimeyes in 2019 and gpt in 2020

62294758
>>62294742
Least schizophrenic LAN shill

62294762
>>62294743
>>62294724
>I'm a big boy, I don't need toys anymore
You're stuck at the mental age of a 14 year old. Vidya is for truly mature people.

62294794
>>62294325
They won't be until nearly 2028 at earliest but the problem then will be that a lot of the companies that support pc building like case maers, fan, makers, psu makers, thermal paste makers etc will be gone. Buy what is cheap now, get your best case, a really really good high wattage PSU, thermal paste, fans etc and hold your money for one- two years for the rest.
>>62294535
>My desktop has been gathering dust for the past 7-8 years. I just use an old laptop for work and music and if I want to play a game I get my 360 or ps2.
I play a lot of XP era games on an XP retro build. Based onn the Ps2 and 360, I do the same but for most it is too late to get a library and they are collectors items now.

62294799
>>62294453
I genuinely see baffling shit like this on /biz/ daily. I'm starting to think being rich makes you retarded.

62294806
>>62294758
Offlinne LANs are the future anon. We both know it.

62294823
>>62294325
The plan is for you to never be able to outright buy your own parts and computer but rather rent the service to play

62294842
>>62294762
There are real bers and zoomer faux nerds. Which one are you? It's the 21 century a man has to have options, money, pussy, drugs, vidya, literature, art, music, film, gardening, hunting, gemology, riding, carpentry, electronics repair., shooting, archery, antqie restoration an collecting, stamp collecting, killing,electronics, programming, driving, motorbikes, falconry, dogs, fitness, martial arts, guitar, piano, harmonica, mandolin, bird spotting, model trains, lego, cooking, writing, academics, casting lea soliders and bulets, sculupture, painting, jewlery making, fine dining, metal work, hiking, military history, tabletop games, photography, war games, chess, thrifting, reading, climbing, tennis, squash, singing, male choir,. Knife sharpening, Making computers, glass blowing, fighting, electronic repair, inciting wars, political philosophy, concert attendence, tracking and killing enemies of your nation, horology, explosives, smallholding, forestry, done racing, learning languages, model aircraft, ham radio, trading crypto stocks and bonds etc etc.

Playing games is just one of those options. They are all fine hobbies, none better that another

62294909
>>62294325
>When are PC parts going to be affordable again?

When openAI goes bankrupt.

62294947
>>62294842
Pretty based, I do most of them. I'd incite wars but I lack the funds. But normalfags (who are female minded) dismiss any hobby that doesn't get them laid and consider the worship of pussy to be the deepest thing in life, bar none. They want to be awash in gash. Women avoid introverted hobbies like the AIDS. Women like money, socializing, displays of psychopathy, extraversion, low IQ, physicality, etc.

62294961
>>62294724
gb2 reddit normalfag

62294979
>>62294947

Man, this argument falls apart the second you stop treating women like a hive mind.

You're basically taking a few traits you've noticed in some people and stretching them across billions of individuals. That's not insight; that's stereotyping with extra steps. There are tons of women who spend their free time reading, gaming, drawing, writing, coding, collecting random niche interests, or disappearing into obscure rabbit holes for hours. The idea that women avoid introverted hobbies "like AIDS" is just obviously false to anyone who's spent five minutes online.

The money and psychopath thing isn't much stronger. If women were only attracted to wealth, status, and flashy antisocial behavior, then shy guys, nerds, artists, academics, and regular dudes would never date. Yet they do, every day. Reality keeps refusing to cooperate with the theory.

And the "normalfags are female-minded" line is weird too. Most people care about relationships because relationships are a major part of human life. That's true for men and women. Having goals outside of niche hobbies doesn't mean someone worships sex. It just means they have different priorities.

The claim that women prefer low IQ is probably the funniest part. In the real world, competence, intelligence, and capability are generally attractive traits. Not universally, because humans are complicated, but far more often than the caricature you're describing.

The biggest problem with this worldview is that it tries to reduce millions of different people into one cynical template. Some women are introverts. Some are extroverts. Some care about money a lot. Some barely care at all. Some like athletes. Some like engineers. Some like artists. Human beings are way too varied to fit into a single edgy slogan.

At some point the theory starts looking less like an observation about women and more like a story you're telling yourself about them.

62294991
>>62294325
PC parts have always been affordable but waitfags expect new GPUs to cost $30

62294992
>>62294325
"Cheap" computing was a market anomaly and we're just returning to normal. Maybe wait 5-15 years and we could see that paradigm return.

62294994
>>62294979

62295011
>>62294992
>market anomaly
Only because despite being cheap to manufacture now, there's absolutely no incentive for the elite to give normalfags fully open hardware and software. The future is fully locked down crap like Androids and iOS. Neutered for "your" safety, of course.

62295101
>>62294961
we use mainchan around here

62298350
>>62294325
Never, which is why I just bought a new PC.
Things will only get worse from here on out with companies like Nvidia renting cloud PC's.

62298359
>>62294325
they are affordable
you are simply poor and should worry more about that than vidya

62298406
>You vill use cloud storage
>You vill use ze cloud gaming service
>You vill own nothing and be happy

Literally weeks before hard drive prices spiked I picked up two 26TB drives for $250 each (now they go for over $800). They’re quite possibly the last drives I will ever (be allowed to) buy.

62298444
>>62294325
Never, goyim gamers are not allowed to own hardware now, you are going to pay a subscription to play in the cloud on top of other subscriptions and microtransactions in order for the game to even run.

62298492
PC Gaming fags really ruined PC gaming. PC gaming was great because it gatekept the retards out. It was actually good that we had shitty ports of gay ass console games. Why do we want these retards in our space?

62298499
Dude I'm using a computer I built in 2015 and am running most modern games at 4K with a decent framerate. The ones worth playing, anyway. There aren't a lot of them. You don't need a PC where everything is current gen top of the line components. Most of the equipment can be old enough that it's fairly cheap, like the CPU, motherboard, RAM, power supply, etc. The only thing that's really important is the GPU and even then you can get by fairly well with a high end card from 1 or 2 generations ago.

item.title: Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? — 30 replies — 15.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet?

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-01 14:29 UTC

OP

Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? Allow me to explain.
Cryptocurrencies support decentralized networks, which are used for data computation and storage. These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
If it's cheaper, why don't corporations use them? One, there are regulations preventing adoption, but CLARITY will change this in the US. Two, corporations have invested trillions into building and maintaining server infrastructure. Liquidation is risky from there point of view because they don't understand decentralized networks. A few brave corporations will lead this great transition, and when they are successful, everyone will follow.
You are in the final window of being early. Don't fuck this up.

Early replies (first 3)

62292772
>>62292765
cope

62292786
>>62292765
How much does 1 Gigabyte on the Bitcoin Blockchain cost?

62292794
>>62292786
>thinks a blockchain is the only data structure for distributed storage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62292827
too bad clarity won't pass

62292899
CLARITY won't change a thing, the public's appetite for crypto is gone.

62292905
>>62292899
CLARITY is not for the public. It's for corporations.

62294414
>>62292765
>These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
Beguiled that you would say this with a straight face.

62294730
It's slowly and steadily coming.
t. takes well over a million in cc payments a year.
The "demand" isnt' from pleb retards swiping their cards. It's from business.
We've all moved to ACH/check for all wholesale tx and it's a big swing the last two or three years.
Tough times make people get pissed.
Businesses will get their cheap processing by 2030 I'd guess.

62295598
>>62294414
What? Are you retarded?

62295608
>>62294730
Exactly.
>Businesses will get their cheap processing by 2030 I'd guess.
I'm anticipating 2028.

62295772
>>62292765
Cool clock Achmed.

62295815
>>62292905
There is no reason whatsoever for them to buy your token. Even Chainlink. They sold you tokens to raise funds, companies won’t need to pay. They made this clear many times including just recently.

62295887
>>62295815
Are you aware of how annoying credit card fees are for businesses? Do you know how much it costs to build and maintain a server room? Crypto saves businesses money.

62295975
>>62295598
Do you have any idea how much data is processed in the world? In the big data world, there's this concept of "scale factor". Scale factor 1 is a gigabyte. So scale factor 1000 would be a terabyte. Things don't even start to get interesting until you're looking at scale factor 10000. A fairly modest, multiple-times-a-day job for a single company is not uncommonly something like a terabyte. Huge companies can run jobs in the petabyte range. Every single one of these is using an insanely optimized query engine (Hadoop, Spark, etc) running across hundreds or even thousands of nodes that are optimized down to the hardware level. Why do they do this? I'll give you a hint - it's not speed they care about. They optimize for cost. They want to get everything as cheap as possible. A couple of retarded desktops spread around the world isn't going to be 'cheaper'. It would take you two weeks just to LOAD a petabyte of memory, let alone process it.

It's hilarious how completely clueless you idiots are about what constitutes "compute". The kinds of things you're thinking about are below toy level.

62296016
>>62295975
I'm aware of the scaling. DePIN is scalable to match and eventually exceed the processing and storage capabilities of servers.
Cute post.

62296032
>>62296016
Ridiculous. A group of randomly connected, disparate pieces of hardware will never even come close to the capacity (or usage price) of dedicated, tuned hardware. You're being defrauded with these promises.

62296045
>>62296032
You are an old man, history will bury you...

62296210
>>62296032
lol

62296324
>>62292765
it took decades for the internet to really be adopted and it still hasn't been fully realized

62296340
>>62295887
Incredible amounts of money over time and it applies to the little guys even more than the big guys. Volume does come through little guys though and they will be included.
Biggest boon in decades for online and local business is coming.

62296419
>>62292827
I am calling it, clarity act will pass, despite the bots online telling you it won't. The BTC halving is under 2 years away, they're squeezing the stock market right now and then smart money rotates elsewhere to make tenbaggers.
All Congress and US politicians are bought by Israel, if you aren't profiting off of this than shame on you. Everyone across the internet will tell you crypto is over forever, and the clarity act won't pass.
Meanwhile look how much politicians own stock in MARA or MSTR. Even trump owns these two.
MARA only owns two assets by the way, bitcoin and Kaspa (Jewish trilemma solving nu coin)

62296587
>>62296419
>then my real life can begin

62296607
>>62292765
Crypto will take many decades to be adopted, and several centuries to become dominant, so we're as early as possible.

We're the bastards that were around when BTC was only $73,000.

62296629
>>62292765
In order to use somebody else's compute, you need to compensate them fairly for it. The math will never work when you consider the price advantage large companies have when buying hardware at scale. Also, they technically already do decentralization with CDNs.

62296638
>>62292765
It doesnt offer a solution to a problem that exists.

62297373
>>62296629
For a huge amount of the business world, actual compute isn't even the real issue. It's interconnects. Data movement. When you're breaking up a 10 terabyte job across a thousand nodes, you are very very rarely bottlenecked by compute. You're bottlenecked by shuffling the data between machines. The whole reason massive datacenters exist is because they can use insane interconnects (1TB, even 10 TB/s fabric). Using retarded internet shitcoin routing mechanisms to attempt this is like using a garden hose to put out a lava flow.

62297920
>>62296638
t. isn't in business and has no money

62297957
>>62296032
i think your missing the point of the tech

62298066
>>62296629
>>62297373
There are P2P relay schemes for efficient data flow. Kademlia routing is one example.

62298770
>>62292765
It has been adopted. Not on a grand scale though. Bitcoin is a deflationary store of value. XMR to buy drugs on the darknet. Stablecoins are used. Perpdex as an online gambling platforms.

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-01 16:17 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

Day 16 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-01 14:13 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62294652
>>62294644

62294655
>>62294644

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294701
same crap every day

62294831
>>62294673
ICP is not risky at all. Too big to fail.

62294975
>>62294831
it failed already 99.99 %
>clear jeet rugpull from the start

62295047
>>62294975
So you’re saying 99.99% down and still in the top 50? Fuck that’s impressive.

62295071
>>62294644
.

62295074
impressive is how many jeets still promote that rugpull

62295078
>>62295074
All 1.5 billion of us saar

62295091
>>62295078
>All

i knew it

>village hungry, dont redeem!!

62295204
>>62295091
you got me saar

62295314
please fuck my ass

62295351
>>62295314
no saar I only shit my ass

62295352
>>62294644
whats even the bull case for this scam coin?

62295365
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62295932
>>62294644
I will be BTFO'ed if this coin hits 20 dollars

62298028
>>62294652

62298559
>>62294652

62298637
>>62294644
Lomesh Dutta is no longer on the dfinity team page. maybe they finally did the needful to #fireLomesh
